last_item: migrate worker pure helpers (internal/server/migrate_worker.go) — failErrorText (:173), boundDiagnostic (:192), unmarshalCounts (:419), toMigrationResponse (:393): on the operator-facing migrate path (every failed migration -> failErrorText; every GET /migrations -> toMigrationResponse->unmarshalCounts) but untested in isolation; they take adversarial input (external pg_restore stderr + JSON blobs round-tripped through SQLite) (band-1 correctness)
last_result: SHIPPED + BUG FIXED: added internal/server/migrate_helpers_test.go (table/subtests) driving all four real helpers. BUG: unmarshalCounts("null") returned nil, not the documented non-nil {} — json.Unmarshal([]byte("null"), &mapVar) sets the map to nil WITHOUT an error, so a JSON-null blob slips past the err!=nil branch and returns the now-nil map, which then serializes back as JSON null on the API (the exact thing the doc says it prevents). Fixed with an 'if out == nil' guard; the JSON-null test reds without it. Tests also pin: failErrorText non-core cause / no-stderr / actionable-append-with-DDL-redaction / no-double-append / non-string-detail / empty-after-sanitize; boundDiagnostic short/at-cap/within-rune-cap/long-ASCII/long-multibyte-rune-boundary(utf8.ValidString)/whitespace-boundary-trim; unmarshalCounts empty/valid/malformed/JSON-null; toMigrationResponse full field mapping with DISTINCT per-field values + malformed-blob->{}. Mutation-proven (in-place apply+revert, NOT git checkout which would wipe the uncommitted fix): newline->space in failErrorText, dropped TrimSpace, Phase:=Status and ProgressDone<->Total cross-wires, and dropping the nil-guard each red the suite. First draft under-asserted (test-skeptic caught 6 unpinned wire fields + 2 separators); strengthened before commit. code-reviewer clean. Gates green (gofmt clean, go vet, go test ./... exit 0, CGO_ENABLED=0 static build); web untouched; e2e not needed (pure unit). Also rejected the pg/hba injectHBARules-self-heal-widened-block item (its normalize-back fix would revert an operator's documented trust->scram hardening = a widening; widened block needs root write to the 0600 file, not an escalation).
notes: Never-ending hardening loop. Bias: (A) prove vibe-coded code actually does what it claims with tests that fail if behavior breaks; (P) preflight every risky/destructive/external op and fail loud+actionable BEFORE acting; (D) tighten defaults + self-heal so a bad config can't wedge Postgres. Re-seed the backlog via a parallel audit panel (Mode S) when it runs thin — this loop does NOT self-complete. Shared defaults live in scripts/ralph/DEFAULTS.md; real sm behavior at /primary01/git/server-management/src/sm/. Run go/make outside the sandbox if snap-confine blocks it.
north_star: An indie hacker runs a rock-solid Postgres entirely from the indiepg UI on best defaults — never loses data, never gets wedged, never confused. Every risky path is preflighted, every failure is loud and actionable, every claim is covered by a test that would fail if the behavior broke.
